title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

title: Johannes Mikkola will defend his PhD thesis
content:
Johannes Mikkola will defend his PhD thesis

mkulmala@helsinki.fi Wed, 20/05/2026 - 11:18

Johannes Mikkola will defend his PhD thesis titled “The influence of topography and black carbon on thermally-driven winds in mountain valleys” on 26th June 2026 at 12:00 in Chemicum A129 (A. I. Virtasen aukio 1, Helsinki).

The defense can also be followed online via this Unitube link:

title: Juhlaseminaari: Ten years of collaborative research-based development of Finnish Teacher Education
content:
Anniversary Seminar: Ten years of collaborative research-based development of Finnish Teacher Education

kakleemo@helsinki.fi Wed, 20/05/2026 - 10:57
The Finnish Teacher Education Forum has been operating for a decade, and it has made a variety of achievements during its existence.

Visioning the future of the Finnish teacher education and concretizing the plans in broad collaboration is an established way of working together in the Forum

title: Taxonomy Recognition Day shines a light on the science behind biodiversity protection
content:
Taxonomy Recognition Day shines a light on the science behind biodiversity protection

johanemi@helsinki.fi Tue, 19/05/2026 - 14:53
The EU-funded TETTRIs project declares May 23rd as an annual Taxonomy Recognition Day and calls on scientists, institutions, and the public to join the #NameItToSaveIt campaign.

title: A Visit of Insight, Collaboration, and Early Numeracy
content:
A Visit of Insight, Collaboration, and Early Numeracy

veikkoak@helsinki.fi Tue, 19/05/2026 - 14:11
The Active Numeracy research group recently had the pleasure of hosting a three-day visit (11–13 May) from Professor Victoria Simms and Professor Camilla Gilmore of the ESRC Centre for Early Mathematics Learning at Loughborough University.

title: Indonesia Inc.: A brief history of entrepreneurship in the name of development
content:
Indonesia Inc.: A brief history of entrepreneurship in the name of development

ehrlichm@helsinki.fi Tue, 19/05/2026 - 12:15
New analysis on the Indonesian Omnibus Law – published on the criticalasianstudies.org Commentary Board. In this new blog post, Moira Moeliono, Tristam Moeliono, Maria Brockhaus, and Grace Wong share some insights in the history of entrepreneurship in the name of development.

You can find the link below.

title: Workshop: Alexis de Tocqueville - French Revolution and Democracy
content:
Workshop: Alexis de Tocqueville - French Revolution and Democracy

aipi@helsinki.fi Tue, 19/05/2026 - 09:47
This workshop is part of the ongoing work of re-reading classic democratic theory, starting from the period of the French revolution and including the work of Jean-Jacques Rousseau, Edmund Burke, and Alexis de Tocqueville. The particular focus of this workshop is on the texts by Alexis de Tocqueville.
